text: Hi, welcome to This is My Architecture. Today we are joined by Pep from ASUN. Welcome Pep.
Thank you for having me. Thank you for being here. So tell us a little bit about
ASUN. What do you guys do? At ASUN we built a SaaS platform to enable the
pharma and biotech industry to use AI and machine learning in compliance. They
are under very strict regulation from organizations like the FDA and the EMA
and this is a crucial point. And compliance is like a key word for your
industry, right? Absolutely. Otherwise they cannot use data, they can build any model
they want, no compliance, no batch can be released. Absolutely. So you provide
platforms to your customers that has the data ingestion, processing, use ML and AI
for getting insights, transformation, visualization, etc. Today we have the
first part which is the ingestion and the processing of this data. Can you walk us
through this architecture? Absolutely. Collecting data is the needed step for
later doing complicated things like AI and machine learning and this is not trivial
neither. The pharma industry leverages sites across the world. They manufacture
millions of billions of pills or vaccines and they have those factors spread across
the world. It's very important to know at every single moment how this is going.
Am I meeting the numbers I need in doses to the market, in pills that need to reach?
This data is located in manufacturing sites that generate terabytes of data
per day. This data gets collected by software agents that they download from the
platform and we leverage IoT to collect this data and upload this data to the cloud.
Why specifically you're using IoT in this context? It's definitely not exclusively for
transmitting data. It really facilitates the orchestration and management of our
software agents that are spread across the world. The IoT allows to see you which is
the status and what you need to do. Then IoT ingest this data into Kinesis. In Kinesis we
can manage all these terabytes of data for later processing. We need to make sure that all the
data that's coming in it's in the proper format. We need to get rid of rogue data. We need to
envelope this data with this compliant envelope to understand every single byte of data. Who, when, where,
what and why it's been used for. Later, Lambda ingests all the data in Elasticsearch. We use it for real-time or near real-time visualization.
But you need persistence and we leverage DynamoDB for that. We store this data in
persistence storage so we make sure we don't lose this data. Data is the most precious thing that our customers give to us.
Absolutely. And what about the data lakes that you build for your customers? How do you do that part on the transformation?

The data lake is a crucial part of the whole schema. All the data needs to be there and needs to be there for years to come. For that we use S3. We store all the data coming in in a readable form of JSON files in S3.

The pharma and biotech companies need to have this data always ready, always readable. Even if everything else disappears, these files need to be readable. But this is probably not the most efficient way to later grab data, to later query this data. So for that we make cyclic processes of the data using glue to transform to formats like Parquet. Binary formats that later
can be queried, recover, recover, and we can get batch information of all of this data.
And we can get batch information of all of this data. And we can get batch information of all of this data.
These Athena calls can be shown using API gateway in websites, dashboards that our customers can have always on, ready available.
And we can get batch information of all of this data.
And we can get batch information of all of this data.
This Athena calls can be shown using API gateway in websites, dashboards that our customers can have always on, ready available.
Absolutely. And get the insights for actually doing the actions that they need. Thank you.
And you work in an industry that is transforming at a fast pace. How do you see the future of this industry?

This industry is changing and it's going to change even more. We see the future with self-driven operation manufacturing sites that they self-optimize and manufacture and even send and call for distribution of the drugs.
Our goal, our mission is to make sure that the right drug reaches the right people that need it at the right time and the right price.
That's a very important aspect. Thank you for sharing this architecture with us today, Pep.
Absolutely. It's been a pleasure.
And thank you for watching. This is my architecture.
